$QCOM CEO said the company’s new AI chip business will become material in 2027.

That is the key line for the rerating case as Qualcomm pushes beyond handsets into AI infrastructure and edge compute.

Bitcoin has sold off around every Fed chair transition since 2014.

Yesterday was Powell’s final FOMC with Kevin Warsh set to inherit 3.5% rates and only one projected cut left in 2026.
Whoops forgot to include the timestamp of my $QCOM thesis share:

$SOFI five years ago:
• $16/share
• 1.5M members with ~$260M gross profit run-rate

$SOFI today:
• $16/share
• 14.7M members with ~$2.4B gross profit run-rate

Same price. Very different business.

Absolutely wild that $META lost $160B in market cap after raising CapEx by just $10B even though the business posted some of its strongest growth metrics in years:

• Fastest revenue growth in ~5 years at 33% YoY
• Ad impressions grew 19%  & Ad pricing grew 12% (rare combo) 
• Reels time spent rose 10% from Instagram ranking improvements
• Q2 guidance came in stronger than expected at up to 28% growth
• Updated ad models drove a 6% boost in landing page view conversions
• Facebook video time rose more than 8% (largest sequential gain in 4 years)
• WhatsApp monetization is scaling with Family of Apps “Other” revenue up 74% YoY

$AMZN AWS operating margins have expanded for four straight quarters & now sit near 38% with Andy Jassy saying custom silicon can add another ~7 points.

The AI economy is moving toward inference & Trainium gives AWS a cost advantage right where the next wave of demand is going.
$META DOWN OVER 10% TODAY

$AMZN Bedrock customer spend grew 170% QoQ and processed more tokens in Q1 than all prior years combined.

AWS is starting to capture the inference workloads that will define the next phase of the AI economy.
$GOOGL Search now generates ~$642M in revenue every single day  🤯

$META confirmed plans to sell investment-grade debt and could raise as much as $25B through the bond sale.

Initial pricing discussions for the longest 2066 notes are around 1.8 percentage points above Treasuries.

Buying $AMZN at ~$270 (~$2.9T market cap) implies you are getting: 

• $20B custom chip business (worth ~$200B)
• $150B ads business growing 23% (worth ~$750B)
• $416B e-commerce business growing 12% (worth ~$1T)
• $129B AWS business growing 28% with 38% operating margins (worth ~$2T)

Add it up and Amazon looks closer to a ~$4T business which would imply ~$365-$370 per share.

$AMZN CEO Andy Jassy said memory and storage costs have “skyrocketed” with memory under the most pressure.

He said there isn't enough capacity for current demand reinforcing why hyperscalers are being pushed toward custom silicon and more cost-efficient infrastructure.

$UBER is launching “Eats for the Way” letting Uber Black riders add coffee, tea or a snack after confirming their ride.

The driver picks it up on the way with the feature rolling out soon in Atlanta, Austin, LA, Philadelphia, San Diego and San Francisco.

$AMKR plans to raise $1B through convertible senior notes due 2031 with buyers able to add another $150M.

Proceeds will fund capped calls to limit dilution with the rest going toward general corporate uses including capex.

OpenAI says it has locked in 10 GW of U.S. AI compute capacity which reaches a goal it originally targeted for 2029 years ahead of schedule.

More than 3 GW was added in the past 90 days alone which includes 2 GW from $AMZN as the AI infrastructure land grab keeps accelerating.

$AMZN, $MSFT, $GOOGL and $META just updated guidance and now plan to spend a combined ~$710B on CapEx this year to build the AI economy: 

• Amazon ~$200B
• Microsoft ~$190B
• Google ~$185B
• Meta ~$135B

This aged well!

Taiwan’s exports reached a record $91.1 billion in March, up 66% YoY and growing at the fastest pace in over 16 years last month on strong demand for AI and HPC products.

$TSM $NVDA $AMD

Google is planning to invest $10 billion in Anthropic with up to $30 billion to follow if the AI firm hits certain performance targets, coming just after Anthropic announced an up to $25 billion investment from Amazon. 

Google Cloud will also provide Anthropic with a fresh 5GW of capacity over the next five years. 

$GOOG $AMZN $AVGO

Google Cloud $GOOG revenue grew 63% YoY to $20 billion, accelerating 15 points from 48% growth in Q4, with backlog nearly doubling QoQ to $460 billion. 

$AMZN $MSFT

Amazon $AMZN reported AWS revenue up 28% YoY to $37.6 billion, accelerating 4 points from Q4 and representing the fastest growth in 15 quarters.

$GOOG $MSFT

Alphabet $GOOG revealed that its first party models are now processing 16 billion tokens per minute, up 60% QoQ.

This would represent an annualized rate of ~8.4 quadrillion tokens. 

$MSFT $AMZN $META

Meta $META boosted its capex outlook for 2026 by $10 billion, now projecting $125-145 billion this year, up from $115-135 billion previously. 

$GOOG $AMZN $MSFT

I remain comfortable saying Nvidia $NVDA will reach $20 trillion by 2030, but investing is all about opportunity cost. 

Read more below about why 2026 may not be as rewarding as previous years.
io-fund.com/ai-stocks/nvidia…

TSMC $TSM is reportedly planning to open a chip packaging plant in Arizona by 2029 for CoWoS and 3D-IC, per Reuters, helping to localize packaging for advanced AI chip production.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO

The new TPU 8i scales to 1,152 chips per pod, up from 256 with Ironwood, offering 331.8 TB of HBM capacity, a nearly 7X increase over Ironwood’s 49.2 TB. 

$GOOG $AVGO $NVDA

Google’s new TPU 8t can scale to 9,600 chips in a pod, offering double the scale-up bandwidth as Ironwood at 19.2Tb/s and 4X the scale-out bandwidth at 400 Gb/s per chip. 

The full pod will offer 121 exaFLOPs of FP4 performance, or nearly 3X Ironwood’s 42.5 eFLOPS.

$GOOG $AVGO $AMZN $NVDA

Google $GOOG recently revealed its eighth-gen TPUs, TPU 8t and TPU 8i, purpose-built for AI training and inference. 

Google says the TPU 8t offers up to a 2.7x performance-per-dollar improvement over the prior generation Ironwood, and both chips offer 2X performance-per-watt as Ironwood.

$AVGO $NVDA $AMZN

SK Hynix reported a more than 5X increase in net profit in Q1 with an operating margin of 72%, more than 14 points above TSMC’s operating margin of 58%.

$TSM $MU $NVDA

OpenAI is targeting having available global compute of 30GW by 2030, a ~15X increase from 1.9GW in 2025, and with more than 8GW of its 10GW commitment from January 2025 already identified.

📺 MARKET REACTION TO MEGA-CAP EARNINGS: TREND STILL INTACT

🔴 Get today's newsletter: t3live.com/rd

The market has been in a constructive uptrend, with $SPY holding above the 8-day, which has been key support throughout the recent rally.

Major overhangs are now behind us: Fed decision (rates unchanged) and the peak of earnings season, with 4 of the Mega Tech names reported. 

Today will show how the market reacts to their results and CapEx projections, but we are still in a healthy bull phase where leadership rotates rather than collapses.

*

I want to share a lesson with you on how I approached $GOOGL and $META going into the earnings.

$GOOGL started as a power play in the underlying trend, but I sold the stock before earnings to eliminate unlimited downside risk. 

Instead of gambling on the binary outcome, I kept exposure through options, where the maximum loss is predefined. 

When Google delivered and the stock rallied sharply after earnings, those options captured the upside — without ever putting the portfolio at risk of a large drawdown. 

That’s a controlled tactic: participate in the move, but define the risk.

Now, $META was approached with a similar idea — a potential catch-up rally after lagging. But again, the structure mattered more than the prediction.

So, I bought a call spread instead of a stock again. The idea was simple: risk a few thousand to potentially make significantly more if the move played out. 

The trade didn’t work — Meta dropped hard after earnings — but the loss was contained and acceptable. If the same idea had been expressed through stock, the loss would have been exponentially larger. 

Same thesis, completely different outcome depending on trade structure.

*

You don’t need to be right every time — you need to survive being wrong. 

Earnings are binary events. You can analyze all you want, but once the numbers hit, the reaction can go either way. 

I don’t try to outguess that uncertainty with size — I engineer trades where the downside is capped, and the upside is asymmetric.

*

I did not participate in $MSFT earnings (except AH trade). Earnings were decent but the stock is down ~$8. Good earnings do not always equal a positive price reaction.

Right now, watch a $420 reclaim, which will signal strength. Breakdown = further weakness.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

Microsoft Q3 2026 Earnings

Key Highlights:
-Microsoft posted revenue of $82.9 billion, up 18% year-over-year, with EPS of $4.27, up 23%.
-AI business hit an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year — the headline number of the quarter.
-Intelligent Cloud was the standout segment at $34.7 billion, up 30%, with overall Microsoft Cloud revenue reaching $54.5 billion, up 29%.
-The commercial remaining performance obligation surged 99% to $627 billion, signaling very strong future revenue visibility.
-Full-year CapEx is now expected to hit $190 billion due to soaring memory costs, reflecting aggressive AI infrastructure investment.
-Net income rose 23% to $31.8 billion, and operating income grew 20% to $38.4 billion.
